common.commonDashboardAllowToolTip: Only include specific Dashboards by providing the name of a Dashboard, or a Regular Expression (REGEX). If not provided, all Dashboards will be included.
common.commonDashboardDenyToolTip: Exclude specific Dashboards by providing the name of a Dashboard, or a Regular Expression (REGEX). If not provided, all Dashboards will be included. Deny patterns always take precendence over Allow patterns.
common.commonDatabasesAllowToolTip: Only include specific Databases by providing the name of a Database, or a Regular Expression (REGEX). If not provided, all Databases will be included.
common.commonDatabasesDenyToolTip: Exclude specific Databases by providing the name of a Database, or a Regular Expression (REGEX). If not provided, all Databases will be included. Deny patterns always take precedence over Allow patterns.
common.commonEnableColumnProfilingToolTip: Generate Data Profiles for the Columns in extracted Tables. Enabling this may increase the duration of the extraction process.
common.commonEnableStatefulIngestionToolTip: Remove stale assets from DataHub once they have been deleted in the ingestion source.
common.commonEnableTableProfilingToolTip: Generate Data Profiles for extracted Tables. Enabling this may increase the duration of the extraction process.
common.commonExtractOwnersToolTip: When enabled, extracts ownership from Looker directly. When disabled, ownership is left empty for dashboards and charts.
common.commonExtractUsageHistoryToolTip: Experimental (Subject to breaking change) -- Whether to ingest usage statistics for dashboards. Setting this to True will query looker system activity explores to fetch historical dashboard usage.
common.commonGithubRepoToolTip: Name of your github repo. e.g. repo for https://github.com/datahub-project/datahub is datahub-project/datahub.
common.commonIncludeLineageToolTip: Include Table and View lineage in your ingestion.
common.commonIncludeTableLineageToolTip: Extract Tabel-Level lineage metadata. Enabling this may increase the duration of the extraction process.
common.commonIncludeUpstreamLineageInReportTool: Useful for debugging lineage information. Set to True to see the raw lineage created internally.
common.commonIngestOwnerToolTip: Ingest Owner from source. Be careful: This cah override Owners added by users of DataHub.
common.commonIngestTagsToolTip: Ingest Tags from the source. Be careful: This can override Tags entered by users of DataHub.
common.commonSchemaAllowToolTip: Only include specific Schemas by providing the name of a Schema, or a Regular Expression (REGEX) to include specific Schemas. If not provided, all Schemas inside allowed Databases will be included.
common.commonSchemaDenyToolTip: Exclude specific Schemas by providing the name of a Schema, or a Regular Expression (REGEX). If not provided, all Schemas inside allowed Databases will be included. Deny patterns always take precedence over Allow patterns.
common.commonSkipPersonalFoldersToolTip: Whether to skip ingestion of dashboards in personal folders. Setting this to True will only ingest dashboards in the Shared folder space.
common.commonStartTimeToolTip: Earliest date used when processing audit logs for lineage, usage, and more. Default: Last full day in UTC or last time DataHub ingested usage (if stateful ingestion is enabled). Tip: Set this to an older date (e.g. 1 month ago) to bootstrap your first ingestion run, and then reduce for subsequent runs. Changing this may increase the duration of the extraction process.
common.commonTableAllowToolTip: Only include Tables with particular names by providing the fully qualified name of a Table, or a Regular Expression (REGEX). If not provided, all Tables inside allowed Databases and Schemas will be included in ingestion.
common.commonTableDenyToolTip: Exclude Tables with particular names by providing the fully qualified name of a Table, or a Regular Expression (REGEX). If not provided, all Tables inside allowed Databases and Schemas will be included in ingestion. Deny patterns always take precedence over Allow patterns.
common.commonTableLineageModeToolTip: Which table lineage collector mode to use. Check out the documentation explaining the difference between the three available modes.
common.commonViewAllowToolTip: Only include Views with particular names by providing the fully qualified name of a View, or a Regular Expression (REGEX). If not provided, all Views inside allowed Databases and Schemas will be included in ingestion.
common.commonViewDenyToolTip: Exclude Views with particular names by providing the fully qualified name of a View, or a Regular Expression (REGEX). If not provided, all Views inside allowed Databases and Schemas will be included in ingestion. Deny patterns always take precedence over Allow patterns.

dbtCloud.dbtCloudAccountIDToolTipOne: The ID of the dbt Cloud account to extract metadata for.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudAccountIDToolTipTwo: This can be found in the URL of your dbt instance: https://cloud.getdbt.com/#/accounts/ACCOUNT_ID/.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudApiTokenToolTipOne: A service account API token for extracting metadata from dbt Cloud APIs. This token must have the privileges required to read metadata (e.g. Metadata Only permissions).
dbtCloud.dbtCloudApiTokenToolTipTwo: For more information about dbt service account tokens, check out the docs here
dbtCloud.dbtCloudCustomMetadataEndpointUrlToolTip: A custom URL used for extracting Metadata. By default, this metadata is extracted from https://metadata.cloud.getdbt.com/graphql. In most cases, users should NOT need to provide this value.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudDataPlatformConnectionTypeToolTi: The type of Data Platform that dbt is connected to.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudDataPlatformInstanceToolTipOne: The DataHub Platform Instance identifier that should be used for the assets extracted from dbt.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudDataPlatformInstanceToolTipThree: Leave this blank if you have not configured a Data Platform Instance when ingesting from the associated Data Platform.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudDataPlatformInstanceToolTipTwo: This is used to correctly connect the metadata extracted from the Data Platform with that extracted from dbt Cloud.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudExtractOwnersToolTip: Try to extract owners from dbt meta properties. Be careful: This can override Owners added by users of DataHub.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudFilterSectionTooltip: Include or exclude specific dbt Node (resources) from ingestion.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudIncludeModelsToolTip: Whether to include extraction of Models or not.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudIncludeSeedsToolTip: Whether to include extraction of Seeds or not.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudIncludeSourcesToolTip: Whether to include extraction of Sources or not.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudIncludeTestDefinitionsToolTip: Whether to include extraction of Test Definitions or not.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudIncludeTestResultsToolTip: Whether to include extraction of Test Results or not.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudJobIDToolTipOne: The ID of the dbt Cloud job to extract metadata for. Choose the job that serves as the primary mechanism for updating your production data.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudJobIDToolTipThree: Ensure that your job enables documentation generation on each run by enabling 'Generate Docs' on dbt Cloud.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudJobIDToolTipTwo: The Job ID can be found in the URL on the Jobs tab of dbt Cloud.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudNodeAllowToolTip: Only include specific dbt Nodes (resources) by providing their name, or a Regular Expression (REGEX). If not provided, all Nodes will be included.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudNodeDenyToolTip: Exclude specific dbt Nodes (Resources) by providing their name, or a Regular Expression (REGEX). If not provided, all Nodes will be included. Deny patterns always take precedence over Allow patterns.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudProjectIDToolTipOne: The ID of the dbt Cloud project to extract metadata for.
dbtCloud.dbtCloudProjectIDToolTipTwo: This can be found in the URL of your dbt instance: https://cloud.getdbt.com/#/accounts/123/projects/PROJECT_ID.

businessGlossary.businessGlossaryCreateTerm_component: Click here to create a new Term. Terms are words or phrases with a specific business definition assigned to them.
businessGlossary.businessGlossaryCreateTermGroup_componen: Click here to create a new Term Group. Term Groups act as folders, containing Terms and nested Term Groups. For example, there could be a PII Term Group containing Terms for different types of PII, such as Email or Phone Number. Learn more about the Business Glossary here.
businessGlossary.businessGlossaryIntro_component: Welcome to the Business Glossary! The Glossary is a collection of structured, standarized labels you can use to categorize data assets. You can view and create both Terms and Term Groups here.

domains.createDomains_component: Click here to create a new Domain.
domains.domainIntro_component: Welcome to DataHub Domains! Domains are collections of related data assets associated with a specific part of your organization, such as the Marketing department. Learn more about Domains here.

entityProfile.documentation_component: You can view and edit an entity's Documentation on this tab. Documentation should provide descriptive information about this data asset. It can also contain links to external resources.
entityProfile.domains_component: You can view and set this asset's Domain here. Domains are collections of related data assets associated with a specific part of your organization, such as the Marketing department. Learn more about Domains here.
entityProfile.entities_component: You can view the Entities that belong to a Container on this tab.
entityProfile.glossaryTerm_component: You can view and add Glossary Terms to this asset here. Glossary Terms are structured, standarized labels for organizing your mission-critical data. For example, if you're marking assets containing PII fields, you might add the Term Email. Learn more about Glossary Terms here.
entityProfile.lineage_component: You can view an entity's Lineage on this tab. Data Lineage allows you to visualize and understand both the upstream dependencies and downstream consumers of this entity. If this tab is disabled, Lineage have not been ingested for this entity.
entityProfile.owners_component: You can view and add Owners to this asset here. Owners are Users or Groups who are responsible for managing this asset.
entityProfile.properties_component: You can view an entity's key-value Properties on this tab. These are sourced from the original Data Platform. If this tab is disabled, Properties have not been ingested for this entity.
entityProfile.schema_component: You can view a Dataset's Schema on this tab. You can also view or add Documentation, Tags, and Glossary Terms for specific columns.
entityProfile.tags_component: You can view and add Tags to this asset here. Tags are labels for organizing your data. For example, you can add a Tag to mark an asset as Mission Critical. Learn more about Tags here.

groups.createGroups_component: Click here to create a new Group.
groups.groupsIntro_component: Welcome to Datahub Groups! Groups are collections of users which can be used to assign ownership to assets and manage access. Groups can be created natively within DataHub, or synced from your Identity Provider. Learn more about Groups here.

homePage.domains_component: Here are your organization's Domains. Domains are collections of data assets - such as Tables, Dashboards, and ML Models - that make it easy to discover information relevant to a particular part of your organization.
homePage.ingestion_component: Start integrating your data sources immediately by navigating to the Ingestion page.
homePage.mostPopular_component: Here you'll find the assets that are viewed most frequently within your organization.
homePage.platforms_component: Here are your organization's Data Platforms. Data Platforms represent specific third-party Data Systems or Tools. Examples include Data Warehouses like Snowflake, Orchestrators like Airflow, and Dashboarding tools like Looker.
homePage.searchBar_component: This is the Search Bar. It will serve as your launch point for discovering and collaborating around the data most important to you. Not sure where to start? Click on Explore All!

welcomeParaph.intro_component: DataHub helps you discover and organize the important data within your organization. You can:

listFeatures.defineOwnership_component: Define ownership and capture knowledge to empower others
listFeatures.gainInsight_component: Gain insights about how others within your organization are using data
listFeatures.quicklySearch_component: Quickly search for Datasets, Dashboards, Data Pipelines, and more
listFeatures.viewAndUnderstand_component: View and understand the full end-to-end Lineage of how data is created, transformed, and consumed

welcomeParaph.pressKey_component: Press  Cmd + Ctrl + T to open up this tutorial at any time.

ingestion.ingestionCreate_component: Configure new Integrations from DataHub to your Data Platforms, including Transactional Databases like MySQL, Data Warehouses such as Snowflake, Dashboarding tools like Looker, and more! Learn more about ingestion and view the full list of supported Integrations here.
ingestion.ingestionRefreshSource_component: Click to force a refresh of running ingestion sources.

lineageGraph.introLineageGraph_component: You can view the Lineage Graph for an entity on this page. Data Lineage allows you to visualize and understand both the upstream dependencies and downstream consumers of this entity. Learn more about Lineage here.
lineageGraph.timeFilter_component: You can click which dates you would like to see lineage edges for on this graph. By default, the graph will show edges observed in the last 14 days. Note that manual lineage edges and edges without time information will always be shown.

policies.createPolicies_component: Click here to create a new Policy.
policies.introPolicies_component: Welcome to DataHub Policies! In most cases, Roles are the best option for granting privileges to DataHub users. When more fine-grained control over user and group permissions is required, then Policies will do the trick. Learn more about Policies here.

roles.introID_component: Welcome to DataHub Roles! Roles are the recommended way to manage permissions on DataHub. DataHub currently supports three out-of-the-box Roles: Admin, Editor and Reader. Learn more about Roles and the different permissions for each Role here.

search.searchResultAdvancedSearch_component: Advanced Filters offer additional capabilities to create more specific search queries.
search.searchResultBrowse_component: Have a clear idea of the schema or folder you're searching for? Easily navigate your organization's platforms inline. Then select a specific container you want to filter your results by.
search.searchResultBrowseTitle: 🧭 Explore and refine your search by platform
search.searchResultFilter_component: Quickly find relevant assets by applying one or more filters. Try filtering by Type, Owner, and more!
search.searchResultFilterV2_component: Quickly find relevant assets with our new and improved filter interface! Our latest update has relocated filters to the top of the screen for ease of access.

users.usersAssignRoleID_component: You can assign Roles to existing Users here. Learn more about Roles  here.
users.usersIntro_component: Welcome to DataHub Users! There are a few different ways to onboard new Users onto DataHub.
users.usersInviteLink_component: Easily share an invite link with your colleagues to onboard them onto DataHub. Optionally assign a Role to anyone who joins using the link. Learn more about configuring invite links  here.
users.usersSSO_component: The preferred way to onboard new Users is to use Single Sign-On.Currently, DataHub supports OIDC SSO. Learn more about how to configure Single Sign-On  here.

onBoarding.policiesIntroRoles: In most cases, Roles are the best option for granting privileges to DataHub users.
onBoarding.policiesIntroFineGrained: When more fine-grained control over user and group permissions is required, then Policies will do the trick.
onBoarding.policiesCreateDescription: Click here to create a new Policy.
onBoarding.rolesIntroRecommended: Roles are the recommended way to manage permissions on DataHub.
onBoarding.rolesIntroThreeRoles: DataHub currently supports three out-of-the-box Roles: Admin, Editor and Reader.
onBoarding.rolesIntroLearnMore: Learn more about Roles and the different permissions for each Role
onBoarding.usersIntroDifferentWays: There are a few different ways to onboard new Users onto DataHub.
onBoarding.usersSsoPreferred: The preferred way to onboard new Users is to use Single Sign-On. Currently, DataHub supports OIDC SSO.
onBoarding.usersSsoLearnMore: Learn more about how to configure Single Sign-On
onBoarding.usersInviteLinkDescription: Easily share an invite link with your colleagues to onboard them onto DataHub. Optionally assign a Role to anyone who joins using the link.
